text.tspan: SHARD (data partition)
u2022 horizontal split of a dataset
u2022 see [sharding](../data/sharding.md)
u2022 typically a database concept
DEPLOYMENT STAMP / CELL (application partition)
u2022 full app stack (web + DB + cache + queue + workers)
u2022 isolation domain: blast radius = 1 stamp
u2022 scale by adding stamps, not growing them
u2022 examples: AWS cells (Route 53, S3); Slack 'cells'; Salesforce 'pods'
u2022 1 stamp may contain N shards
AVAILABILITY ZONE
u2022 cloud provider concept (AWS AZ, GCP zone, Azure zone)
u2022 distinct power / network / fault domain within a region
u2022 typically <1ms latency between AZs in same region
u2022 best practice: replicate across 2u20133 AZs for HA
REGION
u2022 cloud provider concept (us-east-1, eu-west-1, ...)
u2022 geographically distinct location
u2022 10u2013200ms latency to other regions
u2022 unit of geographic distribution / data residency / disaster recovery
GEODE (geographic deployment)
u2022 full app stack in each region
u2192 active-active across regions
u2022 every user hits a nearby instance
u2022 1 geode may contain N stamps; 1 stamp may contain N shards
TYPICAL COMPOSITION (e.g., a large SaaS):
GEODE us-east GEODE eu-west GEODE ap-northeast
3 STAMPS in us-east 3 STAMPS in eu-west 3 STAMPS in ap-northeast
Each stamp = full stack across 3 AZs in that region
Each stamp's DB = N SHARDS (by tenant ID hash within the stamp)
Result: tenant goes to 1 geode u2192 1 stamp u2192 1 shard.
Blast radius per shard, per stamp, per AZ, per geode are all bounded.
Each level is a different unit of isolation/scale/recovery.
